replies.msg:
#include <iostream>

using namespace std;

struct Node {
    Node* next = nullptr;
    int value;
};

struct List {
    Node* tail = nullptr;
    void insert(int);
};

void List::insert(int value) {
    Node node;
    node.value = value;
    if (tail != nullptr) {
        tail->next = &node;
    } else {
        tail = &node;
    }
};

int main() {
    List list;
    list.insert(5);
    cout << list.tail->value << endl;
    cout << list.tail->value << endl;
    cout << list.tail->value << endl;
};


個output係
5
-1002159216
-1002159216
後面兩個應該係overflow左既address
但我明明乜都無做過，點解會自己變左
呢啲可以自己加break point 睇下佢行緊乜
試下用new 去 create 你個node

    } else {

    }
};

int main() {

但我明明乜都無做過，點解會自己變左

replies.msg: In method List::insert(), the variable 'node' is a local variable.  'node' is only valid during the lifetime of the call to insert(). When insert() returns, the local variable 'node' is destroyed with the stack frame.  So you have garbage in your list after the insertion.  The correct way to do this is to use the new keyword to allocate 'node' so that it out-lives the function call.
List::insert 入面果個node, 係call完insert之後就已經消失左

quote.msg: List::insert 入面果個node, 係call完insert之後就已經消失左

replies.msg: 正解！

quote.msg: 試下用new 去 create 你個node

replies.msg: your node is just a local variable of the insert function, its life is ended when the insert function ended.
google下 stack heap memory allocation

樓主應該係覺得
list 係global
佢insert(5)之後
list.tail應該永遠都係value=5, 唔變
?
你寫乜鳩定係我c++比返曬學校
所以應該係樓豬將pointer當咗係array咁做
